glossary.def: The mismatch between an image's low-level pixel values and the high-level meaning we want from it. Two photos of the same object can share almost no pixels while two photos of different objects can look nearly identical, so a useful vision model must learn a representation that is invariant to nuisances like lighting, viewpoint, and clutter. Closing this gap by learning a feature hierarchy from data, rather than hand-designing features, is the core advance of deep computer vision.

The stack of convolution, normalization, and pooling blocks that turns an image into a feature volume, with a small task head bolted on top. It encodes the priors of locality and translation equivariance by sliding small shared kernels across the image, and typically shrinks the spatial grid while growing channel depth. The LeNet-to-ResNet lineage of backbones (AlexNet, VGG, GoogLeNet, ResNet) defined modern vision before the Vision Transformer.

A benchmark of roughly 1.2 million labelled training images across 1000 categories, used in the ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge. It gave the field a shared, hard, large-scale yardstick; AlexNet's 2012 win on it (cutting top-5 error from ~26% to ~16%) is the conventional start of the deep-learning era of vision.

An image classifier that applies a standard Transformer encoder to a sequence of image patches. The image is cut into non-overlapping P x P patches, each is flattened and linearly projected to a token embedding, a learnable [CLS] token and positional embeddings are added, and self-attention does the rest. It removes the convolutional prior entirely, gaining a global receptive field from the first layer at the cost of being data-hungry.

The single image-specific operation in a ViT: each raw P x P x 3 patch is flattened and multiplied by one shared learned matrix E to produce a D-dimensional token vector, equivalent to a convolution whose kernel and stride both equal the patch size P. A 224x224 image with P=16 yields a 14x14 grid, i.e. 196 patch tokens plus the [CLS] token.

The overlap of a predicted region and a ground-truth region divided by their combined area, |A ∩ B| / (|A| + |B| - |A ∩ B|), ranging from 0 (disjoint) to 1 (perfect match). It is the scale-free measure used to decide whether a detection counts (conventionally IoU ≥ 0.5) and, applied per-pixel, to score segmentation masks; averaging it over thresholds and classes gives mean Average Precision.

Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training: a pair of encoders (a vision encoder and a text encoder) trained on ~400M web image-caption pairs so that matching images and captions map to nearby vectors in one shared embedding space, compared by cosine similarity. A symmetric InfoNCE loss pulls true pairs together and pushes mismatched pairs apart, enabling zero-shot classification by comparing an image against text-described labels with no task-specific training.

Classifying an image into categories the model was never explicitly trained on, by writing each candidate class as a text prompt (e.g. 'a photo of a {label}'), embedding the prompts with CLIP's text encoder, embedding the image with its image encoder, and choosing the label with the highest cosine similarity. The classifier is built on the fly from words, which is why it generalizes to open vocabularies and is robust across distribution shifts.

refs.cite: Dosovitskiy, A. et al. (2021). An Image Is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale (ViT). ICLR 2021.
Radford, A. et al. (2021). Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision (CLIP). ICML 2021.
He, K., Zhang, X., Ren, S. & Sun, J. (2016). Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition (ResNet). CVPR 2016.
Krizhevsky, A., Sutskever, I. & Hinton, G. E. (2012). ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks (AlexNet). NeurIPS 25.
Russakovsky, O. et al. (2015). ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge. IJCV 115(3).
Carion, N. et al. (2020). End-to-End Object Detection with Transformers (DETR). ECCV 2020.
Kirillov, A. et al. (2023). Segment Anything (SAM). ICCV 2023.
